name: KeyTrain's Key Training — Identity & Access Security

questions.text: A help desk ticket reports repeated lockouts for one user after they traveled. Logs show failed logins from two countries within minutes. What is the BEST first response?

options.text: Ignore lockouts if the user confirms they remember their password, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable the account permanently without investigation, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Share the user's password hash with the manager to verify identity, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Treat as potential credential compromise: force password reset, revoke sessions, and verify MFA enrollment

questions.explanation: Impossible-travel patterns suggest stolen credentials; reset and session revocation limit abuse while MFA reduces replay risk.
questions.concept: Verify identity with strong, phishing-resistant methods and respond to impossible-travel and lockout patterns.

questions.text: Your org is rolling out passwordless sign-in for contractors. Which control MOST directly reduces phishing of static passwords?

options.text: Shared contractor kiosk accounts, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Annual security awareness posters, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Phishing-resistant authenticators (FIDO2 / passkeys) bound to the identity provider
Longer password complexity rules only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Passwordless FIDO2 resists credential phishing; complexity alone does not stop real-time proxy attacks.
questions.concept: Verify identity with strong, phishing-resistant methods and respond to impossible-travel and lockout patterns.

questions.text: SSO logs show an application accepting SAML assertions after certificate rollover, but only for one legacy app. What should you verify FIRST?

options.text: Whether users cleared browser cache, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.
DNS TTL for the marketing site, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
If the app supports biometric unlock on mobile, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
The service provider metadata and signing certificate trust store match the new IdP certificate

questions.explanation: SAML trust breaks when SP metadata is stale; certificate mismatch is the typical root cause after IdP rotation.
questions.concept: Verify identity with strong, phishing-resistant methods and respond to impossible-travel and lockout patterns.

questions.text: IdP logs show successful logins for a disabled contractor account. What is the BEST remediation?

options.text: Re-enable the account so the contractor can finish work without delay, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Reset only the contractor password while leaving all active sessions valid, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable federation to the account, revoke refresh tokens, and audit provisioning sync jobs
Publish the contractor credentials in the ticket for faster troubleshooting, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Disabled accounts should not authenticate; broken deprovisioning or stale federation mappings need fixing.
questions.concept: Verify identity with strong, phishing-resistant methods and respond to impossible-travel and lockout patterns.

questions.text: An executive refuses hardware tokens but approves SMS codes for banking-style apps. Which risk should you document in the exception?

options.text: SMS messages are always encrypted end-to-end, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Hardware tokens cannot be used on laptops, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
MFA fatigue only affects push notifications, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
SIM-swap and SS7 attacks can bypass SMS OTP without attacking the application directly

questions.explanation: SMS OTP is better than nothing but is not phishing-resistant; SIM swap is a known bypass path.
questions.concept: Multi-factor authentication must resist bypass (SIM swap, MFA fatigue) and protect privileged access.

questions.text: Users report MFA push approval prompts they did not initiate. What is the BEST immediate mitigation?

options.text: Disable MFA globally for 24 hours, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Allow users to approve pushes from unknown locations, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Increase session timeout to 30 days, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Enforce number matching or phishing-resistant MFA and investigate for password spray preceding pushes

questions.explanation: Unsolicited pushes indicate MFA fatigue attacks; number matching and investigating spray reduce success rates.
questions.concept: Multi-factor authentication must resist bypass (SIM swap, MFA fatigue) and protect privileged access.

questions.text: During a tabletop, the team must choose MFA for privileged break-glass accounts. What is the MOST appropriate design?

options.text: Shared TOTP seed in a wiki page, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.
Same SMS OTP as standard users for simplicity, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Hardware tokens stored in a physical safe with dual control and separate from daily user MFA
No MFA on break-glass to speed recovery, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.

questions.explanation: Break-glass accounts need strongest factors and procedural controls; shared seeds defeat MFA purpose.
questions.concept: Multi-factor authentication must resist bypass (SIM swap, MFA fatigue) and protect privileged access.

questions.text: Security wants MFA for all VPN users; help desk reports 40% lockouts after rollout. Best NEXT step?

options.text: Remove MFA from VPN entirely to restore previous login success rates quickly
Allow SMS-only MFA permanently because it has the lowest support burden, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Analyze failure reasons, offer phishing-resistant options, and targeted training for edge cases
Set MFA remember-me to ninety days on all devices without reviewing risk

questions.explanation: MFA rollouts need tuning and user support; abandoning MFA removes a critical control.
questions.concept: Multi-factor authentication must resist bypass (SIM swap, MFA fatigue) and protect privileged access.

questions.text: A junior analyst discovers they can add themselves to a cloud admin group via a misconfigured automation role. What is the BEST remediation sequence?

options.text: Remove excessive permissions from the role, audit CloudTrail for prior abuse, and enforce least privilege on CI pipelines
Delete all automation in the environment, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.
Promote the analyst to admin so they can monitor themselves, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Publish the role ARN in the team chat for transparency, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Fix the permission path, then determine if the flaw was exploited; CI roles are a common escalation vector.
questions.concept: Limit paths to higher privileges in OS, cloud IAM, and Kerberos/AD misconfigurations.

questions.text: Endpoint EDR flags PowerShell downloading a script that modifies local admin group membership. Which indicator BEST supports privilege escalation?

options.text: Scheduled Windows Update reboot, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.
Antivirus definition update, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.
Use of a living-off-the-land binary to run unsigned code that adds a new local administrator
User opened a PDF brochure, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.

questions.explanation: Adding local admins via LOLBins is classic escalation after initial access.
questions.concept: Limit paths to higher privileges in OS, cloud IAM, and Kerberos/AD misconfigurations.

questions.text: A service account in Active Directory has unconstrained delegation. Why is this a critical escalation risk?

options.text: It disables NTLM company-wide, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.
It only affects password expiration policy, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
It encrypts all file shares automatically, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Compromise of the service account can yield Kerberos TGTs usable against other hosts

questions.explanation: Unconstrained delegation allows TGT theft and lateral movement—high severity misconfiguration.
questions.concept: Limit paths to higher privileges in OS, cloud IAM, and Kerberos/AD misconfigurations.

questions.text: Cloud audit shows a Lambda role with iam:PassRole and sts:AssumeRole on admin roles. Risk?

options.text: Removing MFA from the function owner prevents lockouts during deployments, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
AssumeRole is safe when the function code is stored in a private repository
PassRole only affects billing reports and cannot change infrastructure permissions, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Attackers who compromise the function can chain roles to obtain administrative cloud access

questions.explanation: Overly broad execution roles are a common cloud privilege-escalation path.
questions.concept: Limit paths to higher privileges in OS, cloud IAM, and Kerberos/AD misconfigurations.

questions.text: HR terminates an employee, but their SaaS account stays active two weeks because provisioning is manual. Which control gap does this illustrate?

options.text: Overly strict DLP on USB drives, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Insufficient MFA on printers, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Joiner-mover-leaver process failure and lack of automated deprovisioning tied to HRIS
Missing patch Tuesday calendar, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Stale accounts are a top misuse vector; HR-driven deprovisioning closes the window.
questions.concept: Joiner-mover-leaver and access reviews prevent stale, shared, and orphaned accounts.

questions.text: Shared credentials for a production monitoring dashboard are posted in a team channel. What is the BEST replacement?

options.text: Add the password to the email signature for visibility, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable logging to reduce evidence, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Rotate the shared password monthly without changing practice, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Individual accounts with RBAC, optional break-glass procedure, and secrets removed from chat history

questions.explanation: Shared accounts prevent attribution; RBAC and individual identity restore accountability.
questions.concept: Joiner-mover-leaver and access reviews prevent stale, shared, and orphaned accounts.

questions.text: Audit finds contractors using former employees' accounts. What policy enforcement MOST directly stops this?

options.text: Periodic access reviews and automated disablement when employment status changes
Longer minimum password length only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Increasing CCTV retention, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Blocking USB ports on laptops, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Access reviews and lifecycle automation address account sharing and ex-employee reuse.
questions.concept: Joiner-mover-leaver and access reviews prevent stale, shared, and orphaned accounts.

questions.text: Service desk creates generic 'team-admin' accounts shared by three shifts. Primary concern?

options.text: Shift-based sharing is acceptable if the password is rotated every five years
Shared privileged accounts prevent attribution and violate individual accountability controls
Generic names make SIEM correlation easier than per-user accounts would, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Shared accounts improve password memorization and reduce forgotten-password tickets, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Accountability requires per-person identities, especially for administrative access.
questions.concept: Joiner-mover-leaver and access reviews prevent stale, shared, and orphaned accounts.

questions.text: Dark web monitoring shows corporate emails in a breach dump. What is the FIRST technical action aligned to credential abuse defense?

options.text: Block all outbound email for a week, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Publish the dump internally for awareness, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable TLS on the mail gateway, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Force password reset and invalidate refresh tokens for affected identities

questions.explanation: Reset and token revocation contain reuse of stolen passwords and session artifacts.
questions.concept: Assume breached passwords: reset, revoke sessions, scan repos, and detect password spray.

questions.text: Authentication logs show 40,000 failed logins against one legacy IMAP service from one ASN. Which attack type is MOST likely?

options.text: ARP spoofing on the data center VLAN, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
SAST false positive on a web app, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Password spraying against a single protocol before attempting valid pairs elsewhere
Physical tailgating at the front desk, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: High-volume failures on one service suggest spray, often preceding targeted login attempts.
questions.concept: Assume breached passwords: reset, revoke sessions, scan repos, and detect password spray.

questions.text: Developers store API keys in public GitHub repos. Which practice BEST reduces credential abuse long term?

options.text: Disable Git entirely, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Use one global API key for all microservices, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Rename repos to private after launch only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Secret scanning in CI/CD, pre-commit hooks, and short-lived scoped tokens

questions.explanation: Prevention and rotation limit blast radius when keys leak; scanning catches commits early.
questions.concept: Assume breached passwords: reset, revoke sessions, scan repos, and detect password spray.

questions.text: Okta ThreatInsight flags cred-stuffing against your customer portal. FIRST technical response?

options.text: Enable breached-credential detection, step-up MFA, and temporary IP rate limits on auth endpoints
Disable customer logins globally until marketing approves a customer communication, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Lower password length to six characters to reduce typing errors during attacks, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Publish successful login counts on the status page to prove transparency, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Credential stuffing needs rate limits, MFA, and detection—not global lockout without analysis.
questions.concept: Assume breached passwords: reset, revoke sessions, scan repos, and detect password spray.
